name: East African Community
climate: Equatorial to tropical; highland climate in the Rift Valley, semi-arid in northern Kenya and South Sudan, tropical along Lake Victoria

neighbors.name: Horn of Africa
neighbors.relevance: Adjacent conflict-prone region; refugee flows and trade routes via Djibouti and Somalia

neighbors.name: Southern African Development Community
neighbors.relevance: Tanzania and DRC are members of both; overlapping trade and development agendas

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: Parent continental body; EAC is a recognized AU regional economic community

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Access via Djibouti and Eritrea; key export corridor for landlocked EAC members

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: Indian Ocean trade routes connect Mombasa and Dar es Salaam to Asian markets

links.description: All EAC members are AU members; EAC customs union is a building block for AfCFTA
Tanzania and DRC hold dual membership; creates coordination challenges and trade overlap
Djibouti port serves as key export corridor for Ethiopian and South Sudanese goods
Chinese-built Standard Gauge Railway in Kenya and port projects across the region
Port of Mombasa is East Africa's gateway to Indo-Pacific trade; containerized exports to Asia
Red Sea access via Djibouti critical for regional exports; disruptions reroute through Mombasa
